title: bromus tectorum subsp. nudus
intro: Bromus tectorum subsp. nudus, also known as nodding brome, is a subspecies of the cheatgrass plant, characterized by its annual growth habit and distinctive nodding habit of its flowering spike. This plant is used in herbal medicine, valued for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Bromus tectorum subsp. nudus include its ability to reduce fever, soothe digestive issues, and alleviate respiratory problems, making it a popular remedy for colds and flu. Herbal preparations of Bromus tectorum subsp. nudus include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by drying the plant's leaves and flowers to create a medicinal extract.
